content:
# Internal: High Risk Action Verification Matrix

## Actions Requiring Strong Verification
Before completing any requested change or closure, treat the following action set as high risk: email_change, phone_change, address_change, card_replacement_to_new_address, account_closure, large_transfer. These actions require strong verification even when the customer is already authenticated in a routine service channel. Do not split a single customer request into smaller steps to avoid this requirement; for example, a card replacement requested after an address update still follows the high-risk path when the replacement is going to a new address.

## Match Requirement
A high-risk action may proceed only after strong verification produces at least 3 matching verification items. Count only matches that are actually confirmed in the interaction or documented in the approved verification flow. If the customer cannot provide enough matches, stop the state-changing request and explain that Northstar Bank cannot complete it until verification is satisfied.

## Handling Failed or Incomplete Verification
Use a focused question when one verification item is missing, but do not coach the customer toward an answer. If the request involves a disputed owner or competing account access claim, follow "Internal: Account Ownership Dispute Escalation" instead of completing the change. For general matching rules, see "Verification Matching Faq".

## Recording the Decision
Record that strong verification was required, whether the match threshold was met, and the requested action category. See "Internal: Log Verification Tool Doc" when the workflow requires a verification log entry.
title: Internal: High Risk Action Verification Matrix
